channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Survival MMORPG Last Oasis Prepares To Move Onto Next Chapter Update In Latest Season
item.content:encoded:
Exactly two months after the launch of its current Season, the action survival MMORPG Last Oasis is ready to move onto its next chapter as Season 6, Chapter 2 prepares for a May 8 launch. 

The devs have noted that this next major patch will include a full server reset and wipe. With the patch so close, and given the major resets happening, they’ve put out the patch notes with the announcement blog. On top of the bug fixes, many of the changes seem to be rebalances, plus some minor quality of life changes throughout many of Last Oasis’s systems. 

Already, the team has been testing many of these upcoming changes in a PTR; as a result, it seems these changes listed this time will be mostly final. 

Speaking of player testing, they’re asking players to fill out a survey in order to better determine not only what they thought worked well (or didn’t) this past chapter, but also to see what a good “roadmap” and content release schedule would look like.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/survival-mmorpg-last-oasis-prepares-to-move-onto-next-chapter-update-in-latest-season-2000137963
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: In May, The Elder Scrolls Online is Hanging Around the Night Market, Offering Loot, PvP and New Content Preview
item.content:encoded:
Season Zero continues in The Elder Scrolls Online this month, with the newly-opened Night Market event zone already rewarding those that adventure within it. The ZOS team has released an overview of what else we can expect this month.

Despite other activities, it makes a lot of sense that the Night Market is essentially the central feature for May. It just opened on April 29th and runs through June 17th.  You can discover each of the three zones, pledge your loyalty to a faction, and help your faction to win the most Favor overall for special bonus rewards. There are bosses to take down, mysteries to uncover, and a whole lot more. 

Even the latest Golden Pursuits campaign is themed to the new zone. Many of this campaign’s options in that you can complete to hit milestones for new rewards, like a skin, outfit styles, gold, and more, are stuff you can complete in the Night Market. This one also ends on June 17th.

On May 20th, the next dev update, where we will learn about some of what will come in update 51 later this year, is coming.

PvP is the highlight for the Capture the Relic Battlegrounds Weekend, from May 20th through May 27th. Yes, that's a week, not just a weekend, because this one runs as a bigger yet still mini event this month. To take part, get ready for 3v3v3 Battlegrounds matches between the Fire Drakes, Storm Lords, and Pit Daemons. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/in-may-the-elder-scrolls-online-is-hanging-around-the-night-market-offering-loot-pvp-and-new-content-preview-2000137964
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: MagicCon Vegas Preview Panel: Hobbits, Super Heroes, And Alternate Reality Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A Long Expected Party

Today, during the Magic: The Gathering Preview Panel, Wizards of the Coast revealed some of the upcoming cards players can expect to sling in the coming months, including a return to Middle-earth and a reality-fracturing alternate multiverse.

The MagicCon Preview Panel is often the best place to get a sense of what is coming in Magic's upcoming months, and the team at Wizards of the Coast wasted no time, showcasing some of the cards from the upcoming set, kicking things off with The Hobbit. 

Going On An Adventure

While we didn't get an exhaustive look at everything coming with The Hobbit, we did get a sense of at least one returning mechanic: Adventure. First introduced in Throne of Eldraine, Adventure is a mechanic that allows you to cast an instant or sorcery attached to a card, and then cast the full permanent from exile at a later time. Essentially, the creature or permanent involved is "going on an adventure" while in exile, mirroring Bilbo's own experience when he stepped off the threshold at Bag End that fateful day. 

The iconic fifth chapter of The Hobbit, Riddles in the Dark, was also shown off, and it's a nice play on an iconic Magic card, Fact or Fiction, and I think it lines up quite well in portraying the scene through Magic's game mechanics. This was something the original Lord of the Rings: Tales from Middle-earth set did really well: tell Tolkien's story through Magic design that was so thoughtful, it's essentially become my favorite Magic set of all time. 

Iconic characters from The Hobbit also make an appearance, such as the three Trolls in the Trollshaws: Tom, Bert, and William, while Elrond Halfelven, who plays a major role in helping Thorin Oakenshield's company accomplish their quest, returns to Magic in a card that highlights him reading the Ithildin letters on Thorin's map. 

We also see Gollum and his version of The One Ring, which stops short of being a full reprint of the powerful artifact from the original Lord of the Rings Set, but is still an interesting take on the Ring of Power. 

My Precious is a Legendary Artifact Equipment that gives the equipped creature both hexproof and can't be blocked - but at a cost To equip the Ring, you'll need to pay two life in addition to two colorless mana. My Precious also has an Adventure mode, the instant Allure of Power, that is a powerful Black draw spell, letting you draw two cards for just a Black and a Colorless mana, but at the additional cost of two creatures. 

The Dwarves of Thorin's party also appear, including the Dwarven King himself. I was most excited to see The Arkenstone, however, which is another Artifact with an adventure mode. The Arkenstone is a powerful emblem effect, boosting the power of all your creatures while giving its wielder an extra card draw at the end of each turn. The adventure side, Seek the Heart, is a Sorcery that lets you search your library for a Legendary creature, reveal it, and put the card in your hand. 

The iconic dragon, Smaug, also has his own lineup of cards, and rest assured that I will be on hand to pedantically correct your pronunciation of Smaug over the weekend (it's not "smog," to give you a hint). 

Smaug, the Magnificent, is a Legendary Creature - Dragon card that costs 2RR that takes some cues from Goldspan Dragon and other Dragon designs with a Flying, Haste 4/3 that deals damage on attack based on the number of Treasure tokens you control to any target. Smaug also makes a Treasure token at the start of your upkeep. 

The Hobbit will have quite a few different card treatments, which have become standard for Magic sets for about half a decade now, including a beautiful style that evokes the look of an old Fantasy novel from the 70s and 80s. It's surreal to see J.R.R. Tolkien emblazoned across the top of a Magic card, and I absolutely adore this style. 

The Hobbit is also reprinting the iconic The One Ring card, but not in the main set, crucially. It will act as one of the various guest cards making their way into booster packs. We won't see precon Commander decks with this set, either. 

Avengers...Assemble

We also got a fuller glimpse at the upcoming Marvel Super Heroes, celebrating one Marvel hero in particular: The Vision. 

As with Marvel's Spider-Man before it, we are also seeing another Infinity Stone make its triumphant entrance on the Magic: The Gathering stage with The Mind Stone. Like The Soul Stone before it, The Mind Stone is a Legendary Artifact with Indestructible, but takes up the White section of Magic's color pie. Its Infinity ability sees it become a powerful blink effect, exiling a target non-land permanent and bring it back, helping to power strong ETB effects and strategies, and will very likely find itself a Commander staple in any deck running White. 

This set sounds like Head Designer Mark Rosewater's baby, and the card designs reflect that level of attention to both the IP and Magic mechanics that Wizards has been known for. Vision, Synthezoid Avenger, is a great example of this.

Vision is a 3/3 Legendary Artifact Creature that blends Vision's ability to phase through objects in the comics with the phase out mechanic in Magic. It's a brilliant piece of design that helps tell the story of the character through mechanics, while also providing a really cool way to protect Vision if he becomes the target of an enemy spell by phasing him out of the game in response to the cast trigger.

Marvel Super Heroes is almost upon us, as it's the next set on the MTG release calendar, hitting on June 26th. We'll definitely see more in the lead up to release in the next month or so. 

Fractured Reality

The final major set we got a look at today during the Magic Preview Panel is the next in-universe set, Reality Fracture. This set looks trippy as we get best boy Jace Beleren envisioning what the multiverse would look like if molded to Jace's vision. As a result, we are seeing fan-favorite characters in a brand new light.

I think this is best exemplified by Chandra Nalaar, the impulsive fire-wielding Planeswalker from Avishkar. Jace, hoping to make Chandra a little less problematic for the former Guildpact. So we see Chandra, Torch of Defiance (one of the character's most iconic cards) become the ice mage Chandra, Chill of Compliance. 

This is such an interesting take on a character many of us have loved for years, and as a Jace fanboy, I am somewhat conflicted by what he's doing here. According to the set designers, Jace is truly trying to realize his vision of the perfect multiverse, but in doing so, he is taking away the essence of what makes these characters so compelling. It's a real moral quandary that I hope the set explores more in-depth through its storytelling and card design.

We see Garruk, the mighty Green-aligned Planeswalker, who somehow ends up with the Chain Veil, much to Jace's chagrin. We are also going to be seeing a card of his Legendary Axe, simply titled Hunter's Axe, marking the first time this legendary weapon has been printed in Magic. 

Reality Fracture is also doing something incredibly cool with how it is presenting these characters: if you happen to crack one in a booster pack, you'll pull both their normal reality and the fractured reality version of them, at the same rarity. So if you happen to open a pack with Chandra, you'll get both her Torch of Defiance and Chill of Compliance versions. Pretty cool stuff, actually, and something Rosewater mentioned took a lot of work to figure out how to do. 

You can check out the full details of the preview panel in the embedded video below. We'll have more from MagicCon over the weekend, so stay tuned here and our sister site, MTG Salvation. 

Joseph Bradford

Joseph has been writing or podcasting about games in some form since about 2012. Having written for multiple major outlets such as IGN, Playboy, and more, Joseph started writing for MMORPG in 2015. When he's not writing or talking about games, you can typically find him hanging out with his 15-year old or playing Magic: The Gathering with his family. Also, don't get him started on why Balrogs *don't* have wings. You can find him on Twitter @LotrLore



https://www.mmorpg.com/previews/magiccon-vegas-preview-panel-hobbits-super-heroes-and-alternate-reality-revealed-2000137956
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
